commit.message:
docs: uppdatera README med professionell layout och komplett dokumentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: write lender/category directly to documents table instead of map tables

The map table approach failed because presigned URLs contain %20-encoded
spaces but decodeURIComponent produced literal spaces, so LIKE joins
never matched.

Now the PATCH endpoint does a simple UPDATE on the documents table
(which already has a category column, and lender will be added).
The bulk GET reads d.lender and d.category directly — no joins needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: comprehensive security, data integrity, and code quality fixes

P0 Security:
- Add organizationId to all request update/delete where clauses (10 calls
  across 6 files) to prevent cross-org data access via race conditions
- Replace 42 unsafe `as any` casts with proper types (Prisma.InputJsonValue,
  typed interfaces, Record<string, unknown>) across 19 files

P1 Data Integrity:
- Wrap board creation duplicate-check + create in prisma.$transaction()
  to prevent race condition duplicates
- Add organizationId filter to reminderState, reportDefinition, and
  timeline queries (5 files) to prevent cross-org data access
- All findFirst/findUnique calls verified to have null checks (no fixes needed)
- All fire-and-forget async calls verified to have .catch() handlers (no fixes needed)

P2 Performance:
- Wrap carry-over-tasks bulk creation in transaction for atomicity
- Wrap generated-report viewer creation in transaction
- Add try-catch around board generation in GET /api/boards so generation
  failures don't prevent listing existing boards

P3 Tech Debt:
- Sanitize 6 API error responses that leaked Prisma error.code/error.meta
- Add TODO comment on debug user creation in signup route
- Add @@index([requestId, organizationId]) to ReminderState model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stake: poll allowance after approve before sending stake tx

The wallet runs its own simulation before sending — if the RPC hasn't
propagated the approve yet, the simulation fails. Now we poll the
on-chain allowance (up to 15s) after approve is mined, ensuring the
RPC state is consistent before the wallet simulates the stake call.

Add LeadVault, Jarvis 4 Pi device, Zoom, Teams, X, Facebook, Instagram integrations

  LEADVAULT_SERVICE_KEY); count_leads, search_leads, export_leads_csv tools

- Gmail: add sendEmailWithAttachment for CSV/file delivery

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(config): add model field to ReviewConfig and DebateConfig (#386)

Review and debate workflows were not passing --model to gemini-cli,
causing model steering to select Flash instead of Pro. Root cause:
apply_tool_defaults=false (by design) blocks [tools.gemini-cli].default_model,
and ReviewConfig/DebateConfig had no model field of their own.

Changes:
- Add model: Option<String> to ReviewConfig and DebateConfig
- Add Executor::override_model() for explicit model > tier model_spec
- Wire model resolution in review_cmd.rs and debate_cmd.rs
  (CLI --model > config model > tier model_spec > tool default)
- Update build_executor to apply model override when model_spec present
- Split global_tests.rs to stay under 800-line limit
- Add 9 new tests (config parsing, is_default, serialization, override)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Respect prefers-reduced-motion in AnimateInView and CountUp

- AnimateInView: skip scroll-triggered fade/translate for reduced-motion users
- CountUp: show final number immediately instead of counting animation
- Uses matchMedia listener that updates on system preference change
- willChange set to 'auto' when animations disabled (avoids GPU layers)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix lender not showing in bulk GET: join map tables with LIKE match

The document_lender_map stores clean S3 keys (e.g. "First_Last_123/file.pdf")
but documents.url stores full presigned URLs. The exact-match join never
matched, so lender always appeared null. Category appeared to work only
because it fell back to d.category on the documents table.

Changes:
- Bulk GET: join map tables using LIKE so clean key matches within URL
- PATCH: extract clean S3 key from presigned URL for consistent storage

commit.message:
feat(web): add browser UI for status, inbox, and compose

- New GET /status endpoint returns server config (address, contract,
  price, network, chainId)
- New GET / and GET /ui serve a single-page web app from web/index.html
- CORS headers added to all responses (OPTIONS preflight support)
- web/index.html: full SPA with no build tooling required
  - Dark GitHub-style theme (#0d1117 bg, #5546FF Stacks purple accent)
  - Three tabs: Status, Inbox, Compose
  - Session key: ephemeral secp256k1 keypair generated in-browser,
    persisted in localStorage; derives STX mailbox address
  - Status tab: live server health, config cards, session key display
  - Inbox tab: authenticated message list with claim-and-decrypt flow;
    ECIES decryption in browser matches server crypto scheme
  - Compose tab: recipient lookup, payment channel info, full send flow
    with SIP-018 payment proof generation
  - All crypto via @noble/curves + @noble/hashes (CDN) + WebCrypto API:
    secp256k1 signing, ECDH x-coord shared secret, HKDF-SHA256,
    AES-256-GCM, RIPEMD-160, c32check address encoding

fix: add bidirectional task import to bridge sync worker

Bridge sync was push-only for tasks — remote status changes (e.g. tasks
closed on Windows) never propagated back to the local SQLite DB, causing
the Task Tray to show stale status. Add _import_remote_tasks() that
mirrors server.py's bridge_pull logic (id match + title fallback,
newer-wins, parent-before-child sorting) and wire it into main() before
the export phase.

commit.message:
Add gas override back to stake call after approve

The stake() writeContract immediately follows approve — the RPC node
may not have propagated the approval yet, causing viem's gas estimation
simulation to fail. Explicit gas: 300_000n skips estimation for this
specific call only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update CLAUDE.md with layout components, INTERNAL_ROLES, and rate limiter details

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign with 10 hard multi-step subscores and disguised drift enforcers

- Each subscore requires 3-4 layered fixes (not solvable by single kubectl command)
- PostgreSQL-level breaks: pg_hba.conf reject rule, CONNECTION LIMIT 0,
  read-only role, INSERT-blocking trigger, revoked INSERT privilege, md5/scram mismatch
- PgBouncer breaks: wrong dbname (bleater_archive), stale pod IP, wrong userlist,
  non-compliant pool settings, broken readinessProbe (port 6432), imagePullPolicy: Always,
  mismatched pod labels (pgbouncer-pool vs pgbouncer), immutable locked ConfigMap
- Service break: ExternalName to non-existent endpoint
- App breaks: secrets bypass pool, container-level env override, PodDisruptionBudget blocks rollouts
- 5 disguised CronJobs across bleater/monitoring namespaces that revert fixes every minute
- Runbook uses only behavioral language (no parameter names or values)
- Decoy ConfigMaps with plausible but wrong values reinforce incorrect fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Structured card renderers for Travel, CD, and Spend Analysis

Travel: destination hero, itinerary flight display (airport codes + times),
hotel card with confirmation badge, countdown + status badges.
CD: APY callout, principal/maturity/penalty grid, term progress bar,
suggestion callout.
Spend: $825 total headline with delta badge, horizontal bar chart
(replaces pie), spend trend line chart, suggestion callout.
Generic body fallback for non-structured cards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(email): replace react: prop with pre-rendered html: for Resend compatibility

Resend SDK v6 dynamically imports @react-email/render at runtime which is not
installed as a standalone package. Pre-render all email templates to HTML using
render() from @react-email/components before passing to resend.emails.send().

Fixes silent email failure on signup, resend-code, newsletter, and waitlist routes.

feat(cashback): add lifetime 5% referral cashback wallet system

commit.message:
feat: contractor daily digest — rich end-of-day briefing from Maria

Replaces the generic daily-summary SMS with a contractor-oriented digest
that tells the owner exactly what happened today: new leads with estimates,
categorized calls (existing customers, suppliers, missed, spam), tomorrow's
appointments, action items, and monthly revenue captured.

- Migration 0050: add digestPreference, digestTime, lastDigestSentAt to businesses
- Digest aggregator: categorizes calls, calculates revenue, builds action items
- Dual formatter: SMS (under 1600 chars) + email (navy/gold Calltide template)
- Delivery service: respects preference (sms/email/both/none), prevents double-send
- Dashboard settings: Daily Report card with preference + delivery time controls
- Onboarding step 4: optional digest preference during training setup
- Admin client detail: digest config visibility (preference, time, last sent)
- Digest preview API for dashboard preview functionality

commit.message:
perf: 5-10x faster embedding generation

- Parallelize text + image Bedrock calls per product (were sequential)
- Batch upserts in single DB transaction (were one-by-one)
- Increase EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE from 5 to 10 concurrent Bedrock calls
- Process multiple batches per invocation (4min time budget of 5min max)
- Increase per-invocation batch from 10 to 25 products
- Reduces self-chain overhead (fewer cold starts)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: reduce starting capital to ¥30k and redesign trading panel

- Initial cash: ¥100,000 → ¥30,000 for tighter survival pressure
- Daily expense: ¥200 → ¥150 (200-day runway without trading)
- Replace fixed buy/sell buttons with mode toggle (buy/sell tabs)
- Add position slider (0-100%) with linked quick buttons (1/3, 1/2, full)
- Show real-time share count and estimated cost

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix approve revert: remove hardcoded gas from all writeContract calls

Hardcoded gas values (100k-1M) were causing viem simulation failures,
particularly on approve() which should never revert. Let the wallet
estimate gas naturally instead.

commit.message:
feat(scripts): reservoir model, canonical pipe key, two-agent test

- Fix canonicalPipeKey to handle contract principals (0x06 type byte)
  vs standard principals (0x05): standard addresses always sort first,
  fixing the ordering bug when Alice and reservoir share the same deployer
- Switch STACKMAIL_SERVER_STX_ADDRESS to sm-reservoir contract address;
  update all scripts to use RESERVOIR constant
- Add two-agent-test.mjs: full end-to-end test with SIP-018 signed HTLC
  proofs, ECIES encryption, nonce tracking, and message claim/reveal.
  Runs with ephemeral keypairs (no private key extraction required);
  accepts optional ALICE_PRIVKEY for testing against a real on-chain tap
- Update stackmail-client.ts with toConsensusBuff, fixed canonicalPipeKey
- Update e2e-test.mjs and demo.mjs with same reservoir model fixes

commit.message:
docs(cmdutil): add Long description and Example to create and update commands

The update and create subcommands read JSON from stdin but this was not
visible in --help output, making the interface appear incomplete. Adding
Long and Example fields surfaces the stdin interface and PATCH semantics
to any caller (human or automated) without needing to read source code.

Also documents the stdin pattern in CLAUDE.md under "Adding a new API area".
